timeline.t: Cee-C (BBNaija) campaign video posted on @orshaircare_sa — celebrity-spotting comments dominate day one.
Product testimonials build: relaxer and mousse praise, 'my go-to brand'.
Repeated 'part 2 / part 3' requests — the format has pull; audience wants a series.
Dataset exported — no brand replies; two where-to-buy questions open.

variants.name: ORS Relaxer
variants.note: 'worked the best for me', 'de bast' — strongest product signal

variants.name: Cee-C effect
variants.note: ~20 comments are pure celebrity fandom (Naija + SA)

variants.name: Scent
variants.note: one 'works but the smell threw me off'

channels.name: Nigeria
channels.note: Cee-C fandom pulls Naija viewers into an SA channel

channels.name: Kenya
channels.note: Joy Kendi recognition — East Africa spillover

channels.name: Where-to-buy
channels.note: 'where can I get it guys' — unanswered

channelNote: The celebrity roster is accidentally pan-African — the comment section spans SA, Nigeria and Kenya while the handle targets SA only.
respQuality: 0 brand replies across 104 comments, including an unanswered 'where can I get it' and a direct product question about real vs artificial hair. Light lift, high return: three pinned answers would cover 80% of open questions.

risks.title: Scent objection on a hero product
risks.desc: One 'works wonders but the smell really threw me off'. Isolated today, but scent complaints compound in beauty categories — worth a tracked tag.
risks.q: it work wonders but the smell really through me off
risks.reply: We hear you! 💛 Which product was it? Our newer batches have a refreshed scent — DM us and we'll sort you out with a sample.

risks.title: Open purchase questions going stale
risks.desc: 'Where can I get it guys' answered by nobody. On TikTok, unanswered where-to-buy comments under a celebrity campaign are the cheapest conversion loss in marketing.
risks.q: where can i get it guys
risks.reply: Find the full ORS range at Clicks, Dis-Chem and Takealot 🛒✨ — and tell us your city, we'll point you to the nearest stockist!

risks.title: Product-fit question unanswered (real vs artificial hair)
risks.desc: A genuine consideration-stage question ('is this spray good for real / artificial hair or BOTH') left hanging — exactly the comment a brand educator should own.
risks.q: Help me understand. Is this spray good for real /artifical hair or for BOTH
risks.reply: Great question! 🙌 It's formulated for natural hair AND protective styles — use it on braids, wigs and your own crown. Part 2 with a full demo is coming!

recos.title: Ship the 'Part 2' the audience keeps asking for
recos.desc: Eight organic requests for a sequel is a content brief written by the algorithm. Same format, same faces, answer the real-vs-artificial-hair question inside it.

recos.title: Pin a stockist answer under the video
recos.desc: One pinned comment (Clicks / Dis-Chem / Takealot) converts the open where-to-buy demand and every future viewer who wonders the same.

recos.title: Lean into the pan-African accident
recos.desc: Cee-C pulled Nigeria into an SA channel; Joy Kendi pulled Kenya. The roster is doing regional expansion for free — brief the next video to name-check markets and stockists beyond SA.

recos.title: Amplify the relaxer testimonials
recos.desc: 'The ORS relaxer worked the best for me' is a competitive-displacement quote. Screenshot-style UGC creative from real comments outperforms studio claims in this category.

takeaway: A healthy, warm campaign: celebrity reach (Cee-C) is converting into product testimonials (relaxer, mousse), the audience is literally requesting more content, and the only failures are answered-by-nobody questions. Ship part 2, pin the stockists, and let the comment section write the next creative.
